extraction.one_line_summary: WOODS Michael attacked 800m from the summit finish, held off TEUNS Dylan's sprint, and won while VALVERDE Alejandro limited losses with a solo chase effort.
tactical_patterns: VALVERDE rode past roundabout without stopping at summit to minimize time loss, showing superior pacing intelligence under pressure.
YATES Simon accelerated when ARU crashed, exploiting chaos to create gap on QUINTANA who was immediately distanced.
TEUNS Dylan matched every acceleration until final 400m but superior sprint could not overcome WOODS' sustained power differential.
LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel went deep early in breakaway, then faded badly on final climb showing mismanaged effort distribution.

rider_signals.WOODS Michael: positive: attacked 800m from summit on percentage climb and sustained power gap despite TEUNS having superior sprint finish
rider_signals.ARU Fabio: negative: crashed heavily into paving early on stage, stayed down indicating significant injury impact on GC ambitions
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: limited GC loss to 2:41 through tactical solo chase, showed mental presence to maximize effort without stopping
rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: immediately capitalized on ARU crash chaos to create gap, demonstrating sharp race awareness and opportunism
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: tagged off immediately when YATES accelerated during crash confusion, suggesting poor positioning or form weakness
rider_signals.TEUNS Dylan: neutral: matched WOODS repeatedly but lacked finishing power over final 400m despite having better sprint credentials
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel: negative: committed too much energy early in breakaway, then suffered badly on final climb revealing poor pacing discipline
rider_signals.DE GENDT Thomas: positive: took KOM jersey lead through aggressive breakaway, demonstrating classification-specific tactical focus
